title: Store Visit: 7 Steps
description: Standard for an FMCG sales rep store visit: from preparation to report. Condensed version of the classic 10-step call.

steps.title: Pre-visit preparation
steps.content: The night before or in the morning: check the route plan, review account history in CRM (what they bought, when, any debts), prep samples and POSM (posters, shelf-talkers), verify tablet and mobile app. The visit goal = a concrete number (e.g., 'land 3 SKUs of the new flavor'), not 'visit the account'.
steps.key_takeaway: Visit goal is not 'visit' but 'increase order by X'. Without a number, the visit fails.

steps.title: Greeting and contact with decision-maker
steps.content: Greet the clerk, check if the decision-maker (buyer, manager) is in. If not — agree on a return time. With the DM: introduce yourself, use their name, brief small talk (max 30 seconds), transition to business. Don't open with 'we have a new product' — first ask how they're doing.
steps.key_takeaway: DM's name + 30 sec small talk. Then business. Not the other way around.

steps.title: Shelf and stockroom audit
steps.content: Floor walk: photograph your shelf (before touching), count SKUs, price tags, neighboring brands. Stockroom: together with DM, count stock (if no trust — count via the app yourself). Compare with sales history — that's the order baseline. A missing SKU on the shelf = out-of-stock, the #1 lost-sales driver.
steps.key_takeaway: Photo before + photo after. Without photos, POSM reimbursement requests are rejected.

steps.title: Merchandising and POSM
steps.content: Fix the shelf: by planogram, facing the customer, by rotation (FIFO). Check price tags — yours, or competitors on your space? Place POSM: shelf-talker, wobbler, shelf sticker. Rule: POSM works for 30 days, then replace. Photograph the 'after' shelf — upload to CRM.
steps.key_takeaway: 30 days of POSM. Replace after that, or it becomes visual noise.

steps.title: Pitch and building the order
steps.content: Pitch the order, not the product. Not 'we have a new item', but 'I recommend 2 cases — a similar account nearby sold through in 2 weeks'. Use data: regional sales, margin, turnover. Close with alternative questions: 'Shall we do your usual 4 cases or try 6?'. Never 'anything else?'.
steps.key_takeaway: Pitch the order with numbers. 'Try' instead of 'we have a new product'.

steps.title: Handling objections
steps.content: Three main objections: 'no space' (solution — remove slow SKU, add new; show turnover), 'too expensive' (solution — calculate margin in dollars, not percentages), 'won't sell' (solution — check placement; often the shelf is the problem, not the product). Each objection deserves a concrete answer, not an argument.
steps.key_takeaway: No space → rotation. Too expensive → margin in dollars. Won't sell → check placement.

steps.title: Reporting and next-visit planning
steps.content: In the app log: order, before/after shelf photos, outstanding AR, tasks for next visit (bring sample, collect signature, deliver POSM). Agree on the next visit date with DM. Leaving — thank them by name, not 'goodbye', but 'John, I'll be here Thursday at 10, bringing samples. See you!'.
steps.key_takeaway: Next visit booked on a specific date. Without it, you lose control of the account.

quiz.question: What should the visit goal be?
quiz.explanation: A goal without a number can't be measured. No goal = wasted time.

quiz.question: What do you photograph at the store?
quiz.explanation: Before/after photos prove the work and support POSM reimbursement claims.

quiz.question: How to close an order?
options: Wait for the client to say it
quiz.explanation: Alternative-choice questions lift average order by 15-20%.

quiz.question: 'Too expensive' objection — how to handle?
options: Calculate margin in dollars, not percentages
quiz.explanation: Clients live in dollars, not percentages. 40% margin sounds abstract, $1.50 per bottle is concrete.

quiz.question: How to say goodbye to the DM?
options: Name + specific next-visit date + what you'll bring
quiz.explanation: A specific agreement = account retention. A vague goodbye = forgotten.

manager_checklist: Rep arrived with a clear numeric visit goal
Closed the order with alternative-choice questions
Handled at least one objection with a technique

kpi.how_to_measure: CRM: visits with order / total visits
